	{"bbox_2d": [71, 351, 111, 362], "text_content": "2.2.5"},
	{"bbox_2d": [159, 351, 216, 362], "text_content": "DISCH"},
	{"bbox_2d": [159, 368, 917, 393], "text_content": "This input/output pin can be used to implement a discharge path for highly capacitive VBUS line on power system side."},
	{"bbox_2d": [159, 401, 923, 426], "text_content": "When used as input, the discharge is internal and a serial resistor must connected to the pin to limit the discharge current through the pin. Maximum discharge current is 500 mA."},
	{"bbox_2d": [159, 434, 871, 459], "text_content": "The pin can be also used as an open drain output to control an external VBUS discharge path when higher discharge current is required by the application, for instance."},
	{"bbox_2d": [159, 459, 893, 496], "text_content": "The pin is activated at the same time as the internal discharge path on VBUS_VS_DISCH pin. The discharge is activated automatically during cable disconnection, transition to a lower PDO voltage, hard reset and error recovery state. The discharge time is programmable by NVM (see Section 5 Start-up configuration)."},

	{"bbox_2d": [159, 580, 912, 604], "text_content": "This pin is asserted when a valid source-to-sink connection is established. It is also asserted when a connection to a debug accessory device is detected."},
